sn.s: D'Mundart isch nöd nu e Art z'rede.
sn.e: Dialect isn't just a way of speaking.

sn.s: Sie isch en Teil vo de Identität.
sn.e: It's a part of one's identity.

sn.s: Es git Lieder uf Mundart, ganz bekannti.
sn.e: There are songs in dialect, very famous ones.

sn.s: D'Lüt singed sie a Fäschte und im Stadion.
sn.e: People sing them at festivals and in the stadium.

sn.s: Es git au Bücher und Gschichte uf Schwiizerdütsch.
sn.e: There are also books and stories in Swiss German.

sn.s: D'Mundart wird vo Generation zu Generation witergä.
sn.e: The dialect is passed on from generation to generation.

sn.s: D'Eltere redet sie mit de Chind, ganz selbverständlich.
sn.e: Parents speak it with their children, quite naturally.

sn.s: In de Schuel lärnt mer Hochdütsch, dehei redt mer Dialäkt.
sn.e: At school you learn High German, at home you speak dialect.

sn.s: Beidi ghöred dezue, und das isch e Riichtum.
sn.e: Both belong, and that's a richness.

sn.s: D'Schwiizer sind stolz uf ihri Mundart.
sn.e: The Swiss are proud of their dialect.

sn.s: Sie pfleged sie und gänd sie nöd uf.
sn.e: They cherish it and don't give it up.

sn.s: Je meh ich devo verstah, je meh schätz ich's.
sn.e: The more I understand of it, the more I value it.

sn.s: Es isch e läbendigi Sproch mit tüüfe Wurzle.
sn.e: It's a living language with deep roots.

sn.s: Und ich bi froh, es Teil devo z'sii.
sn.e: And I'm glad to be a part of it.
